risk_domain.name: RPC Query Pattern Leakage
risk_domain.description: Privacy leakage through RPC query patterns that reveal user intent, address ownership, portfolio composition, and trading behavior to RPC providers and network observers

capabilities: full observation of all RPC queries from user to provider (RPC provider or network eavesdropper)
timing correlation across queries within a session
cross-session user identification via IP, API key, or query fingerprint
correlation of query patterns with on-chain state changes
aggregation of query metadata across multiple users for pattern analysis
limitations: cannot observe queries routed through Tor or encrypted tunnels without endpoint compromise
cannot distinguish cover queries from real queries if properly constructed
cannot access local execution results (Helios light client)

H1_frequent_balance_checks.name: frequent balance checks
H1_frequent_balance_checks.description: Multiple queries to eth_getBalance for distinct addresses within a short time window, indicating potential portfolio monitoring.

signals.name: frequent_balance_checks
signals.description: Multiple eth_getBalance calls to distinct addresses within a short time window from the same session.

signals.name: repeated_balance_queries
signals.description: Eth_getBalance queries for the same set of addresses at regular intervals.

signals.name: post_transfer_check
signals.description: Eth_getBalance query immediately after an address receives a transfer, indicating possible portfolio verification.

recommendations.description: Implement rate limiting to reduce query frequency.
Log suspicious activity and send alerts for review.
Use an automated tool to apply rate limiting based on heuristics.

H2_repeated_contract_calls.name: repeated contract calls
H2_repeated_contract_calls.description: Repetitive eth_call requests to the same or multiple DeFi contracts, often indicating position monitoring or liquidation risk assessment.

signals.name: repetitive_eth_call
signals.description: Detection of multiple eth_call requests to the same contract method within a short time frame.

signals.name: frequent_balance_checks
signals.description: Repetitive calls to eth_getBalance for multiple distinct addresses from the same origin within a short period.

signals.name: scheduled_calls_pattern
signals.description: Eth_call requests to the same method at regular intervals, indicating periodic monitoring of positions or assets.

signals.name: immediate_balance_query
signals.description: Eth_getBalance calls immediately after a transfer to an address, suggesting the query is related to monitoring a specific transaction.

recommendations.description: Disable eth_call requests to suspicious contracts.
Set up alerts for scheduled calls to specific contract methods.
Implement rate limits on eth_call requests from known malicious origins.
Monitor blockchain transactions for immediate balance queries.
Restrict access to sensitive smart contract methods.

H3_pre_trade_simulation.name: pre-trade simulation
H3_pre_trade_simulation.description: Eth_call to DEX router for swap simulations followed by immediate transaction execution, suggesting pre-trade intent detection.

signals.name: pre_trade_sim_balance
signals.description: Eth_call to DEX router for simulated swaps, followed by immediate eth_sendTransaction.

signals.name: pre_trade_sim_schedule
signals.description: Frequent calls to DEX router for simulated swaps within a short time frame, suggesting pre-trade analysis.

recommendations.description: Block transactions matching the heuristic to prevent unauthorized trades.
Deploy a smart contract monitoring tool to detect and alert on potential heuristics.
Set up thresholds for frequent calls to DEX router to trigger manual review.
Implement rate limiting on API calls to reduce the frequency of suspicious activities.

H4_stealth_address_scanning.name: stealth address scanning
H4_stealth_address_scanning.description: Automated and frequent eth_getLogs queries targeting specific topics of stealth addresses, indicating potential deanonymization attempts.

signals.name: eth_getLogs_frequency
signals.description: Frequent eth_getLogs calls targeting specific topics related to stealth address announcements.

signals.name: repeated_eth_getLogs_interval
signals.description: Repeated eth_getLogs calls to the same contract and topic filters at regular intervals, suggesting automated scanning.

signals.name: narrow_block_range_query
signals.description: Queries with narrow block ranges indicating stealth address scanning for new announcements.

recommendations.description: Limit the frequency of eth_getLogs requests to prevent detection.
Use an automated tool to monitor and block suspicious eth_getLogs queries.
Restrict access to specific topics to reduce the scan range.
Implement network security measures such as firewalls and DDoS protection.

H5_correlated_price_checks_with_h.name: correlated price checks with holdings
H5_correlated_price_checks_with_h.description: Eth_call for token pair prices that match the user's on-chain balance, potentially revealing which tokens they hold or are interested in.

signals.name: correlated_token_price_checks
signals.description: Eth_call requests for token pair prices that match or closely correlate with the user's on-chain holdings.

signals.name: repeated_token_price_checks
signals.description: Frequent repeated eth_call requests to token pair prices, indicating ongoing monitoring of specific tokens.

signals.name: price_query_after_transfer
signals.description: Eth_call for a token pair price immediately after an address receives a transfer, suggesting interest in specific tokens.

recommendations.description: Disable unnecessary eth_call functionality to reduce exposure.
Randomize intervals between price checks to avoid correlation patterns.
Operate through proxy addresses or Tor to anonymize request origins.
Implement rate limiting on API calls to avoid frequent repeated requests.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Test correlated price checks with holdings

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring RPC provider's ability to infer user identity, portfolio, and trading intent from query patterns

methodology.step_1: Simulate N users with known portfolios and trading patterns generating RPC queries through a monitored endpoint
methodology.step_2: Run adversary simulator applying all 5 heuristics to the query log
methodology.step_3: Measure baseline inference accuracy (address linkage, portfolio reconstruction, intent detection)
methodology.step_4: Apply profile recommendations (cover queries, local execution, Tor routing) to all users
methodology.step_5: Re-run adversary simulator on modified query patterns
methodology.step_6: Compare: inference accuracy before vs after mitigation

combined_benchmark.target: Reduce adversary inference accuracy from >90% to <20% for address linkage, <30% for portfolio reconstruction

adversary_levels.name: basic
adversary_levels.description: Balance checks and price correlation only

adversary_levels.name: intermediate
adversary_levels.description: Add position monitoring and pre-trade intent

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including stealth address scanning

templates.risk_assessment: RPC query pattern triggers {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_queries} RPC queries in session. {n_risky} patterns flagged ({pct_risky}%). Leaked information: {leaked_info}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
